catalysts: AI capex guidance for Q4 FY2026
name: Super Micro Computer
risks: Nvidia guidance miss could cascade to component suppliers (15–20% downside)
China export restrictions tightening could reduce backlog visibility
Valuation stretched at 32x forward P/E; any miss triggers mechanical sell-off
thesis: SMCI reports Q3 FY2026 earnings on July 11 AH. AI server demand remains strong with data center capex accelerating; consensus expects $2.82 EPS vs. $2.11 prior year (+34% YoY). Long straddle setup captures volatility spike; historical IV rank at 62% suggests underpriced move relative to 8–12% typical post-earnings move.

catalysts: Immersion lithography demand signals for 3nm/2nm nodes
name: ASML Holding
risks: China trade tensions: ASML faces U.S. pressure on exports (10–15% tail risk)
Macro slowdown in PC/mobile end-demand could defer capex cycles
Customer inventory normalization risk if leading-edge node demand softens
thesis: ASML reports Q2 2026 earnings on July 17 AM (pre-market). EUV lithography orders remain robust; consensus EPS €3.87 vs. €3.24 prior year. Long call spread (buy 500c/sell 520c) targets upside on raised FY26 revenue guidance. Stock trading at 28x forward P/E but deserves premium given semiconductor capex cycle strength through 2027.

catalysts: Data center revenue growth guide contraction from 31% YoY
name: Marvell Technology
risks: AI accelerator demand surprise could reverse bearish setup (10–12% upside gap)
Gross margin beats on pricing power could offset revenue miss
Hyperscaler pre-buys ahead of new architecture launches could surprise positively
thesis: MRVL reports Q2 FY2026 on July 10 AH. Data center revenue guidance likely disappoints relative to 31% YoY growth expectations due to customer inventory correction post-H1 surge. Bear call spread (short 85c/long 95c) captures expected 5–8% post-earnings decline. Implied move is 6% but historical move averages 9%; risk/reward skews bearish.

catalysts: Cloud module ARR acceleration on module consolidation
CRO management commentary on enterprise deal sizes
name: CrowdStrike Holdings
risks: Macro slowdown could defer large deal closures (5–8% downside if guidance cut)

thesis: CRWD reports Q2 FY2026 earnings on July 8 AH. Enterprise security spending remains resilient; consensus EPS $1.09 vs. $0.79 prior year (+38% YoY). Long call diagonal (buy 45 Aug/sell 40 Jul) targets 8–10% move with defined risk. Cloud security TAM expansion supports 25%+ forward guidance; IV at 35% relative to expected 7–9% move favors long vol.

catalysts: July 16 analyst/investor day expected (MI300X roadmap detail)
Data center GPU shipment acceleration (enterprise AI adoption)
name: Advanced Micro Devices
risks: Nvidia pricing power could erode AMD GPU ASPs (8–12% margin compression)
Supply chain constraints on advanced nodes (TSMC capacity limits)

thesis: AMD reports Q2 2026 earnings on July 28 AH (outside 2-week window but traceable pre-announcement signals). Data center GPU revenue poised for 40%+ sequential growth; consensus EPS $2.17 vs. $1.19 prior year. Setup: Wait for July 16 analyst day signals, then enter ratio call spread (long 3x 210c/short 2x 220c) if guidance confidence increases. Current IV at 28% underprices expected 8–10% move.

catalysts: Subscriber net adds beat vs. 4.2M guidance
Ad revenue growth acceleration (Q2 target: +20% YoY)
name: Netflix
risks: Guidance cut on macroeconomic slowdown in core markets (5–8% downside)
Ad CPM compression if recession fears rise
Password sharing crackdown upside potential fully reflected in 100+ P/E
thesis: NFLX reports Q2 2026 earnings on July 14 AH. Subscriber adds expected to beat (consensus 4.7M vs. 4.2M guidance); ARPU growth accelerates on ad tier scale. Long straddle (buy 280 call + buy 260 put) targets 5–6% implied move with skew to upside surprise. IV rank 48% suggests vol underpriced relative to recent 6–8% realized moves on guidance raises.
